text.title: PM 1:1 Template (Engineering / Design) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding PM 1:1 Template (Engineering / Design). Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each 1:1 covers five named sections in order: career / project / craft / blockers / feedback. Skipping a section is the bug — even a 'nothing this week' counts.
rule.rationale: Fixed structure ensures non-urgent topics (career, craft) surface; urgent ones don't crowd them out.

rule.statement: Cadence: weekly preferred, biweekly minimum. Monthly = not a 1:1.
rule.rationale: Trends decay between meetings; biweekly is the upper bound where the rolling log stays useful.

rule.statement: Notes appended to a per-person doc, not scattered. ≥3 meetings before trend is readable.
rule.rationale: Trends appear at meeting 3-5; per-person doc makes them visible.

rule.statement: Feedback section is bidirectional: PM gives + receives. One-way feedback = status meeting, not 1:1.
rule.rationale: Bidirectional feedback is the 1:1's reason for being; without it the meeting is a standup-of-one.

rule.statement: Quarterly: scan rolling log for patterns (recurring blockers, career signals, craft gaps). Adjust agenda.
rule.rationale: Static agenda misses developmental signals; quarterly review keeps the meeting alive.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-pm-1on1-template-engineering-design.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
